retry_reason: The refcount set operation never fails; the only failure is memory allocation which is not a refcount operation failure.
contract_summary: This set-wrapper allocates and initializes an eventfs_inode with an initial reference count of 1 (set via init_ei). The returned pointer represents a reference-owned object; the caller must eventually release the reference by calling an appropriate put function (e.g., eventfs_inode_put). On memory allocation failure, the function returns NULL; the caller should check the return value and may retry the allocation later if memory becomes available.
